mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when rates are already near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not above baseline after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's rolling 2-year rank IC stays below 0.03 in the near-zero rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply above its recent trend, markets price a higher recession probability and weaker earnings, so S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if more than 60% of triggered cases are positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad demand acceleration, raising revenue expectations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise versus their prior readings
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive GDPC1/RSAFS condition does not outperform baseline on 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in INDPRO do not correspond to higher subsequent 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect reverses in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST reflects weakening housing demand and tighter cyclically sensitive credit conditions, which foreshadows lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by neutral or positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the trigger condition fails to separate the bottom-return quintile from the rest.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL inflation falls while FEDFUNDS stays unchanged, real policy eases mechanically and equity multiples expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is unchanged
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with unchanged policy rates do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX gains, or if CPI declines alone explain the effect better than the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: In a falling unemployment regime, continued PAYEMS growth confirms labor-market resilience and supports risk appetite, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is flat or falling over 3 months and PAYEMS is rising
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS/UNRATE condition does not outperform either labor series alone on 5-day SPX returns, or if negative returns dominate in the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: A broad pickup in RSAFS and GDPC1 marks stronger final demand and reduces downside earnings risk, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 60 trading days during post-recession recovery.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous gains in retail sales and real GDP do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the recovery-stage effect disappears outside the designated regime.
